title: Parasyte
reason: Body horror meets moral philosophy, similar thematic weight to Chainsaw Man and Uzumaki. Tight pacing, no filler.

title: Kingdom
reason: Epic historical warfare manga with the same scale and ambition as Vinland Saga. Brutal, strategic, deeply compelling characters.

title: Psycho-Pass
reason: Dystopian psychological thriller with the same cerebral tension as Monster. Explores morality of justice systems in a cyberpunk setting you would love given your Edgerunners S-tier.

title: Ghost in the Shell (1995)
reason: The foundational cyberpunk anime film. If Akira and Edgerunners are S-tier, this is essential viewing.

title: Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex
reason: Expands on the film with slow-burn political mystery arcs. Philosophical depth on par with Evangelion, cyberpunk world on par with Edgerunners.

title: Serial Experiments Lain
reason: Atmospheric, unsettling, existential. If you loved Evangelion's psychological deconstruction, Lain takes that further into digital identity and isolation.

title: Devilman Crybaby
reason: Visceral, emotional, morally devastating. Shares Chainsaw Man's raw energy and Fire Punch's bleakness. Masaaki Yuasa's art direction is extraordinary.

title: Claymore
reason: Dark fantasy seinen with Berserk-level brutality and compelling female leads. Tight 27-volume arc with a proper ending.

title: Battle Angel Alita / Gunnm
reason: Cyberpunk post-apocalyptic manga with the atmospheric world-building of BLAME! and the visceral action of Berserk. A classic.

title: Nausicaa of the Valley of the Wind
reason: Miyazaki's manga is far darker and more complex than the film. Given your Ghibli love and S-tier for Berserk's world-building, this is a must.

title: Ping Pong the Animation
reason: Masaaki Yuasa's masterpiece. Don't let the sports tag fool you; this is a character study on par with Punpun. 11 episodes, not a second wasted.

title: Odd Taxi
reason: Slow-burn mystery thriller with interconnected storylines, like a compressed 20th Century Boys. Brilliantly written, criminally underwatched.

title: Paranoia Agent
reason: Satoshi Kon directing a psychological mystery series. If Perfect Blue and Paprika were A-tier, this is the TV equivalent.

title: Millennium Actress
reason: Another Satoshi Kon film. You rated Perfect Blue and Paprika as A; this is considered his most emotionally resonant work.

title: Tokyo Godfathers
reason: Satoshi Kon's most grounded film. Warm, funny, humanistic. A palate cleanser that still has his signature narrative structure.

title: Texhnolyze
reason: Bleak, atmospheric cyberpunk. If BLAME! is your S-tier manga for its oppressive atmosphere, Texhnolyze is the anime equivalent.

title: Ergo Proxy
reason: Philosophical sci-fi with the slow-burn mystery pacing of Monster. Heavy on atmosphere and existential themes you gravitate towards.

title: Fate/Zero
reason: Dark, morally complex battle royale written by Gen Urobuchi. Mature themes, strategic mind games, stunning Ufotable animation.

title: Kaiji
reason: Psychological gambling manga with unbearable tension. If Death Note's cat-and-mouse was A-tier for you, Kaiji pushes that tension to the extreme.

title: Liar Game
reason: Mind game manga with layered deception. Same intellectual thrill as Death Note but with game theory and trust as central themes.

title: Usogui
reason: High-stakes gambling with brutal consequences. Combines the psychological tension of Death Note with the violence of Gantz.

title: Holyland
reason: Grounded street fighting manga with genuine psychological depth. The protagonist's isolation and growth echo Punpun's themes of alienation.

title: Lone Wolf and Cub
reason: The original samurai epic. If Vagabond was A-tier, this is its spiritual predecessor with even more focused revenge storytelling.

title: Ashita no Joe
reason: One of the most influential manga ever made. Tragic, raw, unforgettable protagonist. The emotional devastation of Punpun in a boxing ring.

title: Blood on the Tracks
reason: Shuzo Oshimi's psychological horror about a mother's toxic love. You gave Punpun S-tier; Oshimi works in the same vein of uncomfortable intimacy.

title: Flowers of Evil / Aku no Hana
reason: Oshimi's earlier work on adolescent darkness. Same suffocating atmosphere as Punpun, shorter and even more focused.

title: Solanin
reason: Asano's most accessible work. You gave Punpun S-tier; Solanin is a shorter, more hopeful exploration of post-university aimlessness by the same author.

title: Nijigahara Holograph
reason: Asano's most cryptic and unsettling work. Non-linear mystery with the atmosphere of Uzumaki. One volume, endlessly re-readable.

title: Innocent
reason: Sakamoto's gorgeous historical manga about the Sanson executioner dynasty during the French Revolution. Art rivals Vagabond, themes are pure dark seinen.

title: Ajin
reason: Immortal humans hunted by government. Tight cat-and-mouse thriller with a brilliant antagonist. Shares DNA with Death Note and Attack on Titan.

title: I Am a Hero
reason: The best zombie manga ever made. Slow-burn start with a deeply flawed protagonist, then escalates into body horror on par with Uzumaki.

title: Inuyashiki
reason: From the Gantz author. Shorter, tighter, more emotionally resonant. Old man gets alien powers; his foil is a psychopathic teenager.

title: Planetes
reason: Hard sci-fi about space debris collectors. Grounded, character-driven, beautiful. The same contemplative quality as Frieren but in a realistic space setting.

title: Jin-Roh: The Wolf Brigade
reason: Bleak political thriller set in an alternate-history Japan. Atmospheric, slow-burn, morally grey. Shares DNA with Monster's sense of dread.

title: Redline
reason: Seven years of hand-drawn animation in one film. Pure adrenaline, stunning visuals. If Akira's animation blew your mind, Redline will too.

title: Sword of the Stranger
reason: The best sword-fighting animation in anime history. If Samurai Champloo was A-tier, this film's choreography surpasses it.

title: Master Keaton
reason: Urasawa's episodic adventure manga. You rated all his other works S-tier; Master Keaton is his most overlooked gem.

title: Look Back
reason: Fujimoto's one-shot about creativity and loss. You gave Chainsaw Man S-tier; this 140-page story hits harder emotionally than anything else he has written.

title: Goodbye Eri
reason: Another Fujimoto one-shot, this one about filmmaking, memory, and unreliable narration. Experimental, moving, and completely unique.

title: Land of the Lustrous
reason: Existential sci-fi about gem beings. Gorgeous art, Buddhist philosophy, and a protagonist's descent that rivals Punpun's darkness.

title: Biomega
reason: Nihei's fast-paced cyberpunk action manga. You gave BLAME! S-tier; Biomega is its more accessible, action-oriented sibling.

title: Baccano!
reason: Non-linear storytelling across timelines with a huge cast. If you appreciate 20th Century Boys' interconnected narrative threads, Baccano does it in 16 episodes.

title: Death Parade
reason: Humans judged after death through psychological games. Each episode is a moral puzzle. Short, impactful, atmospherically gorgeous.

title: Tatami Galaxy
reason: Masaaki Yuasa's visually experimental take on youth and regret. Rapid-fire dialogue, inventive animation, existential comedy.

title: Mushishi
reason: Meditative, atmospheric, and hauntingly beautiful. Each episode is a self-contained mystery. The antithesis of action seinen, but exactly the mood of Frieren.

title: FLCL
reason: Six episodes of pure creative chaos from Gainax. Coming-of-age wrapped in mecha absurdism. If Evangelion was S-tier, FLCL is its unhinged cousin.

title: Ninja Scroll
reason: Classic 90s dark action anime film. Brutal supernatural samurai combat. The same era and energy as 1997 Berserk.

title: Vampire Hunter D: Bloodlust
reason: Gothic dark fantasy film with breathtaking animation. If Berserk's Golden Age atmosphere captivated you, this film delivers similar vibes in 100 minutes.

title: Hajime no Ippo
reason: The definitive boxing manga. Over 1400 chapters of character development, hype fights, and emotional highs. More accessible than Ashita no Joe but just as gripping.

title: Elfen Lied
reason: The manga is far superior to the anime. Dark, psychological, body horror with genuine emotional depth beneath the violence.

title: 5 Centimeters per Second
reason: Early Shinkai. You gave Your Name and Weathering with You S-tier; this is his most emotionally devastating work, pure melancholy.

title: A Silent Voice
reason: KyoAni's masterpiece about bullying, guilt, and redemption. Emotionally on par with Your Name but more grounded and intimate.

title: Megalo Box
reason: Spiritual successor to Ashita no Joe with a cyberpunk twist. Gritty underdog boxing story with stellar music and atmosphere.

title: Dororo (2019)
reason: MAPPA's reimagining of Tezuka's classic. Dark, atmospheric, morally complex. You are already reading Adolf (Tezuka); this is Tezuka's vision with modern production.

title: Great Pretender
reason: Stylish heist anime with Cowboy Bebop's cool factor and globe-trotting energy. WIT Studio animation, jazz-influenced soundtrack.

title: Haikyuu!!
reason: The best sports anime ever made. Don't let the volleyball premise fool you; the character writing rivals anything in your A-tier list.

title: March Comes in Like a Lion
reason: Shogi prodigy dealing with depression and loneliness. Shaft adaptation is also excellent. Same emotional register as Punpun but with warmth.

title: Blue Period
reason: A delinquent discovers passion for art. Genuine, grounded character study about creative obsession. Shares GTO's school setting but with introspection.

title: Heavenly Delusion
reason: Post-apocalyptic mystery with two timelines slowly converging. The same slow-burn mystery structure as 20th Century Boys in a sci-fi setting.

title: Girls' Last Tour
reason: Two girls wander a post-apocalyptic world. Existential, contemplative, surprisingly profound. BLAME!'s lonely atmosphere with a gentle heart.

title: Tezuka: Phoenix
reason: Tezuka considered this his life's work. Spans millennia, explores mortality and reincarnation. You are reading Adolf; Phoenix is his magnum opus.

title: Tezuka: Buddha
reason: Tezuka's retelling of Siddhartha's life. Historical epic with the scope of Vinland Saga and philosophical depth of Monster.

title: Black Jack
reason: Tezuka's dark medical drama about an unlicensed genius surgeon. Episodic moral dilemmas, gorgeous art, morally grey protagonist.

title: Bokura no / Bokurano
reason: Children pilot a mecha, but each battle costs one pilot's life. Evangelion's existential despair taken to its logical extreme.

title: Hellsing Ultimate
reason: Over-the-top dark action with vampires, Nazis, and the Vatican. Gorgeous OVA production values. The same cathartic violence as Berserk.

title: Darker Than Black
reason: Superpowered assassins in a noir-tinged world. Episodic mysteries with an overarching conspiracy. Cowboy Bebop's structure in a darker setting.

title: Kara no Kyoukai / Garden of Sinners
reason: Seven films of dark supernatural mystery by Ufotable. Philosophical, atmospheric, stunning animation. If you liked Fate/Zero's tone, this is its predecessor.

title: Barefoot Gen
reason: Autobiographical account of surviving the Hiroshima bombing. Devastating, historically important. If Grave of the Fireflies was S-tier, this is its manga equivalent.

title: Real
reason: Inoue's wheelchair basketball manga. You gave Vagabond A-tier; Real has the same artistic mastery applied to disability and perseverance.

title: Sun-Ken Rock
reason: Korean gangster manga with jaw-dropping art. Action-heavy with surprising emotional beats. If GTO's delinquent energy was S-tier, this goes harder.

title: Old Boy
reason: The manga that inspired Park Chan-wook's film. Slow-burn revenge mystery, psychological manipulation, morally devastating ending.

title: Banana Fish
reason: Crime thriller set in 1980s New York. Gang warfare, conspiracy, compelling protagonist. Grittier than anything in your current list.

title: Promare
reason: Trigger's wildly animated film. If you appreciated Gurren Lagann's bombastic energy through Kill la Kill's lens, Promare distils it to a single explosive film.

title: Shamo
reason: Disturbing martial arts manga about a teenager who kills his parents. Morally repugnant protagonist, unflinching violence. Dark seinen at its most uncomfortable.

title: Choujin X
reason: Sui Ishida's post-Tokyo Ghoul work. You gave TG B-tier; Choujin X is more mature, weirder, and artistically evolved.

title: Domu: A Child's Dream
reason: Otomo's pre-Akira masterwork. Psychic horror in a housing complex. You gave Akira S-tier; Domu is shorter, tighter, equally unsettling.

title: Gannibal
reason: Rural Japanese village with a cannibalism secret. Slow-burn horror mystery with the same claustrophobic dread as Uzumaki.

title: Tomie
reason: Junji Ito's other iconic work. You gave Uzumaki S-tier; Tomie is his exploration of obsession, beauty, and body horror across connected stories.

title: Gyo
reason: Ito's aquatic horror. More action-oriented than Uzumaki, still deeply unsettling. Mechanical fish invading land is peak Ito absurdist horror.

title: Akudama Drive
reason: Neon-drenched cyberpunk action anime. If Edgerunners was S-tier for its style and energy, Akudama Drive delivers similar visual spectacle.

title: Memories (Otomo)
reason: Anthology film supervised by the Akira creator. Three distinct sci-fi shorts, each with stunning animation. If Akira was S-tier, this is essential Otomo.
